statusLine: EU fuel security under strain as 20 of 27 countries show critical reserves in at least one fuel type, with widespread diesel shortages despite overall safe regional averages.
fullAnalysis:
Despite EU-wide averages appearing adequate at 113 days for petrol, 102.2 days for diesel, and 96.4 days for jet fuel, the distribution reveals significant vulnerabilities across member states. A striking 20 of 27 countries are experiencing critical shortages in at least one fuel category, with diesel showing particular weakness as 15 countries fall below the 90-day mandatory minimum.

Romania faces the most severe crisis, with all three fuel types in critical status - petrol at just 40.4 days, diesel at 30.2 days, and jet fuel at 76.4 days. Poland and Luxembourg also show critical levels across all categories. The aviation sector appears especially vulnerable, with 17 countries below safe jet fuel reserves, including major economies like Germany (64.7 days) and France (67.5 days).

The fuel price landscape reflects ongoing market pressures, with Brent crude at $102.98/barrel - a dramatic 42.1% increase since late February's Middle East escalation. This surge is translating into consumer pain, with EU average petrol prices at €1.824/L and diesel at €1.863/L. The Netherlands faces the highest petrol costs at €2.388/L, while Malta enjoys the lowest at €1.34/L.

While recent strategic reserve releases have helped maintain regional supply security, the data suggests these measures are masking deeper structural challenges. The uneven distribution of reserves across member states, combined with elevated crude prices and geopolitical tensions, indicates continued pressure on European fuel security in the coming months.
keyPoints: 20 of 27 EU countries show critical reserves in at least one fuel type, despite safe regional averages
Diesel shortages most widespread with 15 countries below 90-day mandatory minimum
Romania in severe crisis across all fuels: petrol 40.4 days, diesel 30.2 days, jet fuel 76.4 days
Aviation sector under pressure with 17 countries showing critical or warning jet fuel levels
Brent crude surged 42.1% since February escalation to $102.98/barrel, driving consumer price increases
